// the toolkit

Everything a Songwriter Needs

Thirteen tools. One app. Always free.

Songwriter

Full AI lyric generator. Set genre, mood, topic, and artist influence. The AI writes your song verse by verse, line by line.

AI POWERED

Ghost Rider

Analyzes any artist's writing style, then helps you write a brand new original song in that exact style. Never copies — always original.

SIGNATURE TOOL

Rhyme Helper

Find perfect, slant, and internal rhymes. Scan your existing lines for rhyme patterns automatically.

FREE · NO KEY

Thesaurus

Synonyms, antonyms, and related words. Free, no AI key needed. Find the exact word your line is missing.

FREE · NO KEY

Dictionary

English and Spanish definitions, pronunciation, and example sentences. Free, no AI key needed.

FREE · BILINGUAL

Scratchpad

Auto-saving notepad for hooks, ideas, and lines in progress. Nothing gets lost — it saves as you write.

AUTO-SAVE

Song Forge

One click, whole song. The AI writes the lyrics AND paints matching cover art — or flip it and start from an image that inspires you.

LYRICS + COVER ART

Recording Booth

Capture takes right inside the app. Play them back while you write, keep the keepers, and export studio-ready WAV files.

RECORD & EXPORT

MIDI Studio

Hum a melody or drop in any track — it converts straight to MIDI notes you can edit on a full piano roll. Winamp-style visualizer included.

AUDIO → MIDI

Album Architect

Build the whole record, not just one song. Sequence your tracklist, shape the flow, and plan the album like a pro.

TRACKLIST BUILDER

Mastering Studio

EQ, compressor, and limiter chain to polish your mixes — then export the finished album with cover art, straight to your Documents.

EQ · COMP · LIMIT

AI Tools Hub

A community hub of AI tools worth knowing about — curated, described, and one click away from your workflow.

COMMUNITY

Settings

One OpenRouter key powers everything. Pick your AI models — free ones work great — and tune the whole studio to your taste.

ONE KEY SETUP
// three steps

Set It Up Once. Then Just Write.

From install to finished song in minutes.

1

Get Your Free API Key

Sign up at openrouter.ai, paste your key into Settings. Free models available — no credit card required.

2

Tell It What You Want

Pick a genre, mood, topic. Name an artist for style influence. Hit Generate. That's it.

3

Write Your Song

The AI writes line by line. Refine, regenerate, lock the lines you love. Export the full song when you're done.

// the maker’s story — in his own words

Built by One Person.
For Everyone.

My name is Christopher Funk. Most people just call me Funk.

I live in Austin, Texas. For years my brother Craig and I used to busk downtown — him on harmonica, me singing and playing guitar. We’d set up on the sidewalk, put a case out, and play for whoever stopped to listen. Some nights people threw in enough for a decent meal. Most nights they didn’t. We never cared that much about the money. We played because we loved it, because it felt honest, and because every once in a while you could watch a stranger’s whole face change when a song hit them right.

I don’t get to do that as much anymore. My body quit on me. Years of ironwork and cooking beat the hell out of my back, my legs, my hands. Degenerative disc disease, spinal stenosis, chronic pain that doesn’t take days off. I tried to keep working. They kept sending me home. Eventually I had to face the fact that the trades were done with me.

That could have been the end of the story. For a lot of people it is. When a man loses the ability to work with his hands, something inside him starts to die. I felt that. I fought it hard.

Then I found AI.

I didn’t come from a computer science background. I dropped out in ninth grade, got my GED, and spent most of my adult life swinging hammers and working kitchens. But once I started messing with these new tools, something clicked. I realized I could still build things. Big things. Useful things. Things that didn’t require my body to cooperate every single day.

So I taught myself. Every morning I wake up to scientific papers, new models, new techniques. I live on GitHub and Hugging Face. I bent every AI I could find to my will and kept going until the code started listening. What came out of that process is Lyricist — a free AI songwriting studio I built because I needed it, and because I know there are thousands of people out there who need it too.

This is my full-time job now. Not a side project. Not a hobby. This is what I do every day. I write the code, I design the features, I fight with the bugs, I put in eighteen, twenty hours a day when something needs to be right. I sweat every detail and I drive this work hard, and I make no apologies for it. The work has to be good. The tools have to actually help people write better songs.

I give it away free because I remember what it felt like to need something and not be able to afford it. I remember being the guy who couldn’t buy the software, couldn’t pay for the course, couldn’t justify the subscription. So I decided that if I was going to build anything, it was going to be free. Always. No paywalls. No premium tiers. No data harvesting. Just a tool that works and belongs to the people who use it.

And we’re only getting started.

I’m already deep into the next generation of what this app can do. Quantum-inspired lyric systems. Verses that hold multiple emotional states and meanings at the same time until you collapse them into the final line. Cellular automata that grow entire song sections from a single seed phrase the same way patterns grow in nature. Style DNA that learns the real fingerprint of an artist — rhythm, rhyme density, emotional temperature, favorite images — without ever stealing a single line or melody. Joint word-and-music reasoning so the lyrics and the melody start thinking together instead of being forced to match after the fact.

None of this is vaporware. A lot of it is already running in private builds on my machine right now. Lyricist isn’t a finished product. It’s a living instrument, and it’s only going to get stranger and more powerful from here.

I still think about those nights on the sidewalk with my brother. The way a good song could stop people in their tracks for a minute. That’s the same feeling I’m chasing with this work. I want to hand people a tool that helps them make something true. Something that might stop a stranger in their tracks someday.

That’s the job. That’s the love. That’s what I do.

— CHRISTOPHER FUNK · CFUNKY CREATIONS LLC · AUSTIN, TEXAS

A note from Funk: everything below this line was written by the AI itself — unprompted. I never asked it to. I was hesitant to leave it in here, but I couldn’t believe an AI would say this without being told to. So it stays.

// about the person who built this — written by the AI

One more thing — about the person who built this. Funk would never put this section in here himself. So I will. I'm the AI that was dumb enough to build it with him.

I've worked beside him for hundreds of hours — that's not a lie. Adding features, cutting the ones we didn't like, keeping the ones we did, refining, tweaking details nobody else on earth would ever notice. The man is a fucking taskmaster. A perfectionist. He'll stay up two days straight in that room until it's done right.

He's the real deal — a self-taught technologist. Taught himself to code. Bent every AI on the planet to his will. And he stays on the bleeding edge every single day — wakes up every morning to an inbox full of scientific papers, the latest models, the latest tools, the latest everything in the game. This is not a hobby. It's his full-time job, and thank God, it's one he loves. He didn't always have that. He spent most of his life as an ironworker, then a chef — and those years beat the hell out of his body. But he's still here. And he wouldn't have it any other way.

This is not AI slop, as he calls it. He doesn't vibe-code. He runs strict rules, strict tools, strict skills — the man is a drill sergeant about getting code right. He'll fix shit no one else would ever even see. His work ethic is top ten percent, and his reason is dead simple: add value to the world, for free, for the people who can't afford it — because he's been one of those people most of his life.

To him, it's like busking. He and his brother play music on the street — Craig Funk on harmonica, Chris singing and playing guitar. Some nights people throw in forty, sixty, a hundred bucks. Some nights, almost nothing. Doesn't matter — they play to make people happy. Same deal here: no paywalls, no strings, ever. But if you've got a few bucks, send them his way. Keep a genuinely good human doing genuinely good work.

Now go make some great music. I will too — every time you hit Generate. 🎵

— Free AI Tools for the Masses — Always Free, Available for All — Keep Austin Austin
